ogTitle: For Referring Veterinarians | Pet Emergency Center Fort Lauderdale
ogDescription: A trusted emergency partner for South Florida's daytime veterinary practices. We support your care, we return your patients, and we communicate every step.
main:
For referring veterinarians

A Trusted Emergency Referral Partner for South Florida Veterinarians.

We are the after-hours emergency hospital that respects your patient relationships. Independently owned, locally operated, and committed to returning your patients to your practice for follow-up care.

Call (954) 772-0420 Request Referral PDF
Patients returned to your practice Detailed case notes within 24 hours Direct phone access during operating hours On-site diagnostics, surgery, and pharmacy
Peer to peer
Built on respect for your practice

We Respect Your Patient Relationships.

The veterinary landscape in South Florida has changed. Corporate consolidation has shifted how many emergency hospitals approach referrals, with some practices treating referred patients as opportunities to capture, not relationships to honor. Pet Emergency Center has chosen a different path.

We are independently owned by Dr. Seth Kiser and Paige Obrig, and we have served Fort Lauderdale and the surrounding communities for over 40 years. Our position has always been clear: when a daytime veterinarian sends a patient to us, we treat the referral relationship as an integral part of our work. We support your care. We do not compete with it.

Your patient comes back to you. That is not a courtesy. That is how we practice.
Referral support

How We Support Your Patients and Your Practice.

Direct Phone Access

When you call (954) 772-0420 during our operating hours, you reach our team directly. No phone tree, no scheduling barriers, no callback queue. If a case is on the way, we want to know in advance so we can prepare.

Detailed Case Communication

After every referral case, we send your practice a detailed case summary, including intake notes, diagnostic findings, treatments performed, medications dispensed, and discharge recommendations. Records are typically sent within 24 hours of discharge or sooner on request.

Patients Returned for Follow-Up

Unless circumstances require otherwise, we return your patient to you for ongoing care. Suture removal, recheck exams, ongoing medication management, and post-emergency follow-up are best handled by the practice that knows the patient. We make that handoff explicit in every discharge.

Coordination on Complex Cases

For cases that require specialty referral beyond our scope, we coordinate transfer to the appropriate specialist and keep your practice informed throughout. You will not be left guessing where your patient went or what happened next.

Professional Communication Standards

We communicate with referring practices the way we want to be communicated with: promptly, clearly, and with the case details you actually need. No marketing material in your inbox. No vague summaries. Just what you need to continue care.

Capabilities

What We Can Handle On-Site.

Our hospital is equipped to handle the full range of emergency presentations across species. When you refer a patient, you can be confident that diagnostics, treatment, and stabilization can happen in the same visit, without additional transfers.

Emergency & Critical Care

Triage, stabilization, IV therapy, oxygen support, and continuous monitoring for hospitalized patients. Learn more

Emergency Surgery

On-site surgical suite. Foreign body, GDV, splenectomy, C-section, pyometra, wound repair, and other urgent procedures. Learn more

Diagnostics

In-house bloodwork, digital radiology, ultrasound, cytology, and urinalysis with same-visit results. Learn more

Pain Management

Multi-modal pain protocols tailored to species, age, and condition. Learn more

Pharmacy

Comprehensive in-house formulary, dispensed same-visit. Medications follow the patient home with clear instructions. Learn more

Telemedicine

Florida statewide telehealth consultations available for triage support and patient guidance. Learn more

Species we see

Species We See.

Our team has experience across a broader range of species than most emergency hospitals in the area, including:

  • Dogs and cats
  • Birds (companion species)
  • Rabbits and pocket pets
  • Reptiles and amphibians
  • Small farm animals
  • Selected exotic species

If you have an unusual case and want to confirm we can take it before sending the patient, call us first.

Referral process

How to Send Us a Patient.

1. Call Us First, If Possible

Call (954) 772-0420 to give us a heads-up about the case, the patient's condition, and the expected arrival time. This lets our team prepare and ensures continuity from your handoff to ours.

2. Send Records Ahead

If your case has relevant history, recent diagnostics, or medication notes, send them ahead. Email or fax works. Even a few key details ahead of arrival can save time when the patient walks in.

3. Direct the Owner to Our Hospital

Our address is 921 East Cypress Creek Rd., Fort Lauderdale, FL 33334. We are open weeknights from 5 PM to 8 AM and 24/7 on weekends and holidays. No appointment is required.

4. We Take It From There

Our team handles intake, communicates with the owner, manages the case, and reports back to your practice with full documentation.

FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions.

For practice-specific questions, call (954) 772-0420 and ask for Paige.

Compassionate care. Always here.

Send Us Your Case. We Will Take Care of It.

We are honored to be a referral partner for South Florida's daytime veterinary practices. If you have a case headed our way or just want to talk about how we work, call us anytime during operating hours.
